name: Web App
purpose: Next.js App Router application with the main lobby, localized rules dialog, settings dialog, shared audio controls, locale switching, the single-player Domino Poker game UI, and the multiplayer lobby + game table (MP zone under components/mp + lib/mp; renders server snapshots only, sends intents).
risks: AppShell owns lobby/game state, locale, audio settings, and screen transitions.
The lobby has separate desktop wheel and compact viewport layouts; keep both flows wired to the same selected round count and start-game handler.
DominoPokerGame has timer-driven AI turns and delayed trick completion; UI changes can affect turn flow.
GameDialogs, PlayerSeat, InfoPanel, DominoTileView, and LobbyWheel are UI extraction boundaries; keep core rules and deck/dealing logic out of these components.
The main browser smoke test covers lobby start, bidding, tile play, optional number selection, and first trick completion; keep it stable when UI labels or dialog semantics change.
The layout regression test verifies Lobby and Game viewport containment at desktop/tablet sizes; keep it aligned with the current no-phone-reflow layout contract.
Use the shared Dialog component for modal semantics, focus trapping, Escape wiring, and focus restoration instead of duplicating dialog markup.
The dialog accessibility test covers modal semantics, initial focus, Tab focus trapping, Escape handling, focus restoration, and mandatory bid behavior.
Local storage access should go through safeStorage and remain best-effort when browser storage is malformed, blocked, or throwing.
The game table uses uniform contain scaling of the fixed 1920x1080 stage; phone portrait reflow is intentionally out of scope.

The main-lobby multiplayer entry is now enabled: the mode wheel has a circular 'Lobby' button on the bottom (multiplayer) half mirroring the single-player Play button, and the compact panel's multiplayer button is enabled; both call onStartMultiplayer to switch AppShell screen to 'mp-lobby'.

Client MP zone (Phase 8, apps/web/lib/mp/, kept under lib/ to match the existing no-src layout) holds the non-UI networking foundation, never single-player rules. clientId.ts: getOrCreateClientId() returns a stable per-browser UUID persisted in safeStorage under 'domino-poker-client-id' (the private connection id sent in HELLO; only the server displayId is shown publicly). clientView.ts: reduceServerEvent(view, event) is a PURE reducer folding all 12 ServerEvent types into ClientView {connection, identity, lobby{rooms,onlineCount,chat}, room, game{snapshot,seq}, lastError} - no rules logic (game.snapshot is the server's authoritative truth; GAME_EVENT only advances seq for gap detection; GameSnapshot type is derived as Extract<ServerEvent,{type:'STATE_SNAPSHOT'}>['snapshot'] to avoid importing core subpaths). apps/web depends on @domino-poker/shared (protocol contracts, single source of truth) and has vitest (apps/web/test/mp/). MultiplayerClient.ts is the transport-agnostic WebSocket client: HELLO handshake, auto-reconnect + PING, folds ServerEvents through reduceServerEvent, and exposes typed send methods. createRoom sends {visibility, numberOfRounds, fillWithBots}; submitBid/submitMove read roomId+turnId from the view. Recovery: seq gap or post-reconnect WELCOME in a room triggers REQUEST_SNAPSHOT(lastSeq); protocol mismatch stops reconnect. webSocketAdapter.ts is the only real browser WebSocket wrapper. useMultiplayer.ts builds/connects the client and exposes {view, actions}. MultiplayerLobby.tsx is LIVE-wired: ConnectionBanner, public/private room columns + onlineCount, working chat (React text, never innerHTML), and L2 CreateRoomDialog using the shared Dialog/focus trap with visibility, rounds, and AI-fill controls. Join/L3 waiting room/L4 code join and MP game table are still next slices. NOTE: server LobbyChat stores raw text; React text rendering is the XSS layer. Phase 12 4-player-test fixes: (1) errors are TRANSIENT everywhere - reduceServerEvent clears lastError on ROOM_LEFT (so a game error like NOT_YOUR_TURN 'does not own the current turn' cannot leak into the lobby after the game), and MultiplayerLobby auto-dismisses the top error note (6s) + chat error note (4s); MpGameTable already shows errors as a self-dismissing toast. (2) MpGameTable auto-closes the declared-number dialog when table.turnAction !== 'move' (i.e. the viewer's turn ended, e.g. via the server 10s timeout auto-play) so a late declared-number choice can't fire a stale-turn rejection.

name: Core Rules
purpose: Pure TypeScript Domino Poker rules, tile handling, bidding, legal-play validation, scoring, round state, game state, and CPU AI decisions.
risks: Rule changes can alter scoring, legal-play validation, dealer rotation, and AI decisions.

`shuffleAlgorithm.ts` is shared by SP `shuffleSet()` and MP seeded shuffle, but RNG/deal ownership stays separate: SP defaults to Math.random; MP supplies `createSeededRng(seed)` and keeps deterministic server-side setup.
Public game setup validates dealerIndex and custom round decks, including exact tile count, duplicate normalized tiles, and raw pip values from 0 to 6.
`getInvalidMoveReason` is the structured source for UI invalid-move messages; keep `canPlayTile` as the boolean compatibility wrapper.
AI tile selection uses core `isStrongerTileWithContext` with AI-specific ace context; preserve existing AI heuristic behavior when refactoring comparator code.
Docs contain some stale scoring details; prefer current tests and implementation when they conflict.

The package emits ESM files through a postbuild import-fix script (scripts/fix-esm-imports.cjs) that recursively walks dist/ and appends .js (or /index.js) to every relative import/export specifier, including the multiplayer subfolder; keep it generic rather than hardcoding module names.
The multiplayer barrel is exposed to other workspaces through the `@domino-poker/core/multiplayer` subpath export so MP stays a separate entrypoint from the single-player root export.

name: Core Multiplayer Helpers
purpose: Isolated multiplayer helper layer for deterministic setup and future command/event wrappers without changing single-player rule flow.
risks: Must not alter single-player shuffle/deal behavior.
MP state/player/turn fields belong in multiplayer wrappers, not in core single-player types.

CREATE_GAME / createMultiplayerGameSetup accept an optional humanSeatIndices (Phase 6.7 multi-human support): after createNewGame it remaps each player's isAI/playerType by seat index (humans = given indices, rest bots) WITHOUT touching the shuffle/deal/rules. Omitted => historical default {seat 0 human, 1-3 bots}, so the simulator and existing callers are unchanged. This is the only place the lobby's human/bot composition enters the engine; out-of-range indices throw.

MP applyCommand currently implements CREATE_GAME, START_TURN, TURN_TIMEOUT after deadline via autoBid/autoMove, AUTO_MOVE_FALLBACK when no legal timeout move exists, timeout inactivity metadata updates with AUTO_PLAY_ENABLED when auto-play activates, on-time activity inactivity-score reduction, PLAYER_DISCONNECT (human-only, sets connectionState disconnected and emits PLAYER_DISCONNECTED) and PLAYER_RESUME/DISABLE_AUTO_PLAY manual-control restoration with AUTO_PLAY_DISABLED for manual disable, START_NEXT_ROUND via deterministic startNextRound, REQUEST_SNAPSHOT, SUBMIT_BID via makeBid, SUBMIT_MOVE via playTile, and completed-trick delegation via completeTrick.

PLAYER_DISCONNECT only flips connectionState (rejecting bots); inactivity/auto-play escalation still happens solely through TURN_TIMEOUT, and PLAYER_RESUME remains the reconnect that resets score/status/connection. replay.ts already ignores PLAYER_DISCONNECTED as a transport-only event.

MP TurnState allowedActionTypes uses command names (SUBMIT_BID, SUBMIT_MOVE, TURN_TIMEOUT); START_TURN generates deadlineAt from injected now, and SUBMIT_BID/SUBMIT_MOVE reject now > deadlineAt with ACTION_TOO_LATE.
MP autoBid is deterministic and must choose only from legalBids while reusing the existing core makeAIBid heuristic.
MP autoMove is deterministic, must choose only from legalMoves, and passes a player-scoped core state to selectAITile so opponent hands are not visible to the auto-action heuristic.

MP inactivity metadata is separate from core domino scoring, preserves bot seat status, persists across START_NEXT_ROUND, increments on timeout, decrements after on-time human activity without going below zero, and disables auto-play only through explicit resume/disable commands; auto-play state transitions must emit explicit MP events for future replay.

MP event coverage audit verifies implemented state-changing applyCommand paths emit consecutive events; CREATE_GAME uses seed/setup as the replay starting point, and REQUEST_SNAPSHOT/rejected commands remain eventless.
MP replay rebuilds core GameState from ordered MultiplayerEvent streams and a deterministic seed; it must stay in multiplayer and must not change core single-player rule APIs.

MP snapshots build privacy-safe views: createPublicSnapshot omits every player's hand tiles (keeping only handCount) and createPlayerSnapshot adds only the viewer's own hand plus the active turn deadline; the public PublicPlayerSnapshot type structurally has no hand field so opponents' tiles can never leak, and both snapshot variants cover all four seats including AI bot seats.
MP legalMoves and legalBids are implemented as read-only helpers; state changes must still go through applyCommand.
MP invariants are implemented for diagnostics/assertions in tests and simulations; they must remain read-only.
MP phase-flow coverage plays a full one-round game through applyCommand without WebSocket and verifies deterministic final results for a fixed seed.
Deterministic shuffle/setup must remain seed-driven and must not call Math.random.

name: Shared Multiplayer Contracts
purpose: Transport-neutral WebSocket protocol shared by server and client (Phase 6.1/6.2/6.3). Contains the protocol version + compatibility check, structured error codes/payload, Zod schemas for all client->server messages (discriminated union), the public room DTO types (RoomSummary/RoomView/etc.), and TypeScript types for all server->client events (ServerEvent union).
risks: Keep transport-neutral: protocol/message/DTO types only, never game rules (those live in packages/core).

Client->server messages are untrusted input - keep strict Zod validation (parseClientMessage); the gateway returns INVALID_MESSAGE on failure. SUBMIT_MOVE carries move={tile,declaredNumber?}, distinct from the core command shape (the gateway translates). Server->client events are trusted server output, so they are TypeScript types (not Zod).

Public room DTOs (RoomStatus/RoomVisibility/SeatKind/RoomSeatView/RoomSummary/RoomView) and room-round constants live here and are the single transport contract; RoomSummary/RoomView include numberOfRounds. LobbyManager imports + re-exports DTOs and keeps only the internal Room/Seat (with playerId) local.
serverEvents.ts embeds core payload types (PlayerSnapshot/MultiplayerEvent) via `import type` only (erased) so the runtime dependency stays zod-only.

Build uses the composite tsconfig.json (referenced by the root); typecheck uses tsconfig.typecheck.json (noEmit, includes tests, paths->core source). zod is a runtime dependency; relative imports must keep .js extensions (no postbuild fix script here).

name: Multiplayer Server
purpose: Authoritative multiplayer server workspace. Phase 5 (room engine + lobby) is complete: RoomEngine (single-writer per-room dispatch over core.applyCommand, mocked turn timers), LobbyManager (rooms, seats, host, bot-fill, host migration, FSM, TTL), RoomManager (membership, routing, room<->engine wiring, reconnect snapshot, lifecycle), DisplayIdRegistry identity, and structured lobby error codes. Phase 6.4 adds the WebSocket gateway (net/): transport-agnostic WebSocketGateway (HELLO handshake, protocol-version check, JSON+Zod validation, identity binding via SessionManager - the original Phase 6.4 net/SessionRegistry has been removed as dead code, PING/PONG) plus a ws transport adapter mounted on the same HTTP server/port via `upgrade` (decision B, path /ws). Phase 6.5 wires CoreMessageRouter to RoomManager for the lobby commands (LIST_ROOMS/CREATE_ROOM/JOIN_ROOM/LEAVE_ROOM/FILL_SEATS_WITH_BOTS): the requester gets the matching reply event and any room-list change broadcasts LOBBY_STATE via the gateway's GatewayHub; ALREADY_IN_ROOM is enforced by RoomManager. Phase 6.6 adds the lobby chat (LobbyChat) wired through SEND_CHAT + connect/disconnect lifecycle hooks (CHAT_HISTORY on join, LOBBY_STATE/onlineCount on connect+disconnect). Phase 10.1-10.2 add the DB-agnostic persistence layer (storage/): StoragePort is an async (Promise-based, SQLite/PostgreSQL parity) contract over serializable DTOs (match start+seed, append-only event log, match result, basic player stats, lobby chat history); SqliteStorage implements it with the built-in node:sqlite (no native deps) using INSERT OR IGNORE / upsert idempotency and a WAL-mode file DB; PostgresStorage implements the same contract via pg, JSONB payloads, BIGINT ms timestamps, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING idempotency, and atomic ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE stats increments (updated_at uses max/GREATEST under concurrent writes); config.readDatabaseUrl accepts SQLite paths/:memory:/file: prefixes and PostgreSQL URLs; openStorage routes by DATABASE_URL scheme. PostgreSQL multi-instance foundations are wired: RoomLeaseStore + RoomOwnershipGuard use `room_leases` with atomic acquire/renew/release SQL and CoreMessageRouter enforces ownership before room-scoped commands; DurableSessionStore uses `player_sessions` so reconnectToken/displayId are shared across instances; PostgresEventBus uses `server_event_fanout` + LISTEN/NOTIFY so broadcast/player/supersede fanout reaches sockets on other instances without republish loops. PostgresEventBus prunes old fanout rows, reconnects the LISTEN client after listener errors, catches up fanout rows missed during reconnect windows, skips local self-notification lookups, and writes INSERT+NOTIFY as one SQL CTE. Active room state still lives in the owning Node process, so full multi-instance room failover still needs room-affinity/owner routing or explicit state rehydration/command forwarding. Phase 10.3 wires match-lifecycle persistence into the live flow: RoomEngine emits events to MatchPersistence, RoomManager records match starts/events, LobbyChat persists/hydrates chat history, and GAME_OVER updates player stats via StoragePort.incrementPlayerStats. PostgreSQL mode is covered by `npm run test:postgres:docker` (disposable Docker DB) plus optional TEST_POSTGRES_DATABASE_URL tests.
risks: RoomEngine.dispatch is the single state-mutation path (single-writer); never mutate room state elsewhere. It serializes commands through a re-entrancy queue, assigns a contiguous room-level seq per event, keeps an in-memory event log, caches idempotency by playerId+requestId, and overrides each time-sensitive command's now with the server clock (server is the time authority). Acceptance is decided by result.errors.length===0, NOT by nextState (Phase 6.7 fix): core fail(...) keeps nextState=current state, so illegal commands were previously mis-accepted with their errors dropped. getGameState() exposes the trusted full MP state for the GameDirector (never sent to clients).

Turn timeout uses an injected TurnTimerScheduler (ManualTimerController for tests; SystemTurnTimerScheduler = real setTimeout in production, Phase 7) scheduled at deadlineAt+1 on TURN_STARTED and cancelled when the turn ends; stale timeouts are guarded by pendingTurnId and core turnId validation. Phase 7 integration: RoomEngine.onTurnTimeout fires AFTER a self-fired timeout's accepted dispatch with its events; RoomManager wires it to handleTurnTimeout -> advanceGame -> gameUpdateSink. The sink (set via setGameUpdateSink) is provided by index.ts as publishGameUpdate(gateway, rooms, ...), so timer-driven updates reach seated humans with the same delivery as client-initiated moves. publishGameUpdate lives in net/gameUpdateDelivery.ts and is shared by the router (deliverGameUpdate) and the timeout sink; GatewayHub was extracted to net/GatewayHub.ts to avoid an import cycle. TURN_STARTED already carries turn.deadlineAt/startedAt and the GAME_EVENT wrapper carries serverNow (Phase 7.2 - client countdown data).

Server consumes core via @domino-poker/core/multiplayer and the protocol/DTOs via @domino-poker/shared. Typecheck (tsconfig.json, noEmit) resolves BOTH to source via `paths` and intentionally has NO project `references` (references force built dist and cause TS6305/TS2307); the composite build (tsconfig.build.json) keeps the shared reference and resolves to dist, so `npm run build` must build core+shared first (workspace order handles it). LobbyManager imports room DTO types from @domino-poker/shared (type-only, erased) and re-exports them.
Server-side multiplayer must remain authoritative and must not leak private GameState snapshots (getSnapshotForPlayer uses createPlayerSnapshot; getPublicSnapshot omits hands).

DisplayIdRegistry (Phase 5.5) assigns public `#??` ids derived deterministically from playerId via the core MP seeded RNG, unique with salt-based collision regeneration and stable per session; the same format covers bots, and full playerId/clientId is never the public identifier. LobbyManager now wires displayId into seat names; clientId one-room-at-a-time (ALREADY_IN_ROOM) comes with RoomManager.

LobbyManager (Phase 5.2/5.4) owns the room registry and FSM (WAITING->STARTING->IN_GAME->FINISHED->DESTROYED): createRoom (code + createdAt + expiresAt = +1h, host at seat 0, visibility, numberOfRounds), listRooms (WAITING/IN_GAME only, private rooms flagged), assignSeat (max 4 seats empty|human|bot), fillSeatsWithBots (host-only, WAITING-only, deterministic bot ids), host migration on leave (next human by seatIndex; DESTROYED when no humans), startGame (host-only, needs 4 filled + >=1 human), and destroyExpired (TTL sweep that skips IN_GAME). Public views expose displayId and room metadata, never playerId; injected clock + room id/code generators keep it deterministic in tests.

lobbyErrors.ts defines LobbyError + LobbyErrorCode (ROOM_NOT_FOUND, ROOM_FULL, GAME_ALREADY_STARTED, NOT_HOST, ALREADY_IN_ROOM, FORBIDDEN, plus ROOM_NOT_JOINABLE/NOT_ENOUGH_PLAYERS/PLAYER_NOT_IN_ROOM); reused by LobbyManager and RoomManager.

RoomManager (Phase 5.3) wires LobbyManager + per-room RoomEngine: tracks clientId->room membership (one room at a time, ALREADY_IN_ROOM), createRoom accepts visibility/numberOfRounds, join by id (public) or code (private requires code), startGame creates the room's engine and dispatches CREATE_GAME (gameId == roomId) passing humanSeatIndices and room numberOfRounds, routeMessageToRoomEngine forwards only to the matching room and rejects mismatched gameId, getSnapshotForClient maps seat index -> core playerId and rejects non-members, and destroyFinishedRoom does FINISHED->DESTROYED + drops engine + membership. Each room gets its own TurnTimerScheduler.

Seat<->core-player mapping is by index: lobby seat i corresponds to core game player id String(i+1) (matches createNewGame). The clientId->playerId protocol translation (HELLO) and GAME_OVER auto-detection arrive in Phase 6.

GameDirector (Phase 6.7, src/rooms/GameDirector.ts) drives one room's game loop over RoomEngine (single-writer). advance() repeats: roundEnd->START_NEXT_ROUND; no active turn->START_TURN; active bot turn (MP status==='bot')->deterministic autoBid/autoMove via core; active human turn->stop with awaitingHuman=true; gameEnd->stop with awaitingHuman=false. It carries NO game rules (bot choices delegate to core autoBid/autoMove; all mutations go through engine.dispatch) and the server is the time authority so commands pass now:0 (RoomEngine.withServerNow overrides with clock()). Every orchestrated dispatch is checked for accepted (throws on rejection) with a MAX_LOOP_STEPS guard, so failures fail-fast instead of hanging. advance() is idempotent once awaiting a human (no dispatch). It reads RoomEngine.getGameState() (trusted full MP state incl. all hands; NEVER sent to clients). Wiring START_GAME->advance + STATE_SNAPSHOT/GAME_EVENT delivery, and Phase 7 timer-fired-timeout->advance, come next.

WebSocket gateway (Phase 6.4, net/) keeps the golden rule: handlers carry NO game rules, only validate + delegate. WebSocketGateway is transport-agnostic (built on the GatewayConnection interface) so it is unit-tested with fake connections; the real ws socket is wrapped only in wsTransport.ts. Incoming pipeline per frame: JSON.parse -> parseClientMessage (Zod) -> HELLO handshake (isProtocolCompatible; mismatch => ERROR PROTOCOL_VERSION_MISMATCH + close 4001) -> identity required -> MessageRouter.route. SessionManager (sessions/, Phase 9.1, replaces the old net/SessionRegistry) binds connection->SessionIdentity (sessionId/playerId/displayId/reconnectToken) and is DURABLE: the reconnectToken persists per clientId across disconnects (until release) so a reconnecting HELLO is validated against it. register(connId, clientId, token?) -> {identity, isReconnect, replacedConnectionId} | {token_mismatch}; an unknown clientId mints a fresh token, a known clientId must present the exact token (else FORBIDDEN + close 4004), and a second live connection for the same clientId supersedes the first (close 4003 -> single active socket, no double control). ownsPlayer/isActiveConnection/hasActiveConnection + onlineCount (active sockets); it shares the DisplayIdRegistry with the lobby so WELCOME and seats show the same displayId. release(playerId) drops the durable token + displayId; it is wired via RoomManager.setMemberDepartedHandler -> WebSocketGateway.releaseSession (offline-guarded) so a session is freed when a player loses room membership while offline (leave/forfeit/room destroyed) - NOT on disconnect, because the token must survive disconnect for reconnect grace + wrong-token impostor rejection. Decision B: ws runs on the SAME http server/port (4000) via the `upgrade` event at path /ws; needs @types/ws (dev). Heartbeat (Phase 6.8): each ConnectionContext tracks lastSeenAt (updated on every inbound frame); sweepHeartbeats() (deterministic, uses the injected clock) closes connections silent for >= pingIntervalMs*missedPongThreshold (defaults 15000*2; close code 4002) via a shared teardown() path reused by transport close (idempotent). wsTransport runs the real setInterval(gateway.sweepHeartbeats, gateway.getPingIntervalMs()).unref() and clears it on wss close. CoreMessageRouter now handles ALL 12 post-handshake message types (PING, the Phase 6.5 lobby commands, the Phase 6.6 SEND_CHAT, and the Phase 6.7 START_GAME + SUBMIT_BID/SUBMIT_MOVE + REQUEST_SNAPSHOT + PLAYER_RESUME); the switch is exhaustive (a `never` check guards future additions). PLAYER_RESUME (handlePlayerResume): requireMembership -> core PLAYER_RESUME dispatch with requestId=`resume:${sessionId}` (idempotent per connection) -> on accept, deliverGameUpdate broadcasts PLAYER_RESUMED + a fresh personalized snapshot to seated humans; on idempotent replay, resync the requester only; reject -> FORBIDDEN (or ROOM_NOT_FOUND if no game yet). snapshotFor() centralizes building a personalized STATE_SNAPSHOT event (reused by SUBMIT replay, REQUEST_SNAPSHOT, and PLAYER_RESUME). Phase 9.2/9.3-a reconnect orchestration (server-driven, no client message needed): onConnected -> restoreRoomOnReconnect: if the (re)connecting clientId still sits in a room (membership persisted across the disconnect), send ROOM_JOINED (room view -> client view.room) and, if IN_GAME, dispatch PLAYER_RESUME -> deliver PLAYER_RESUMED + a fresh personalized STATE_SNAPSHOT (which now carries the active turnId so the reconnected client can submit). onDisconnected(hub, serverNow, disconnected?) -> if the player went FULLY offline (the gateway passes the identity only when no active socket remains; a superseded socket passes undefined) and sits in an IN_GAME room, dispatch PLAYER_DISCONNECT (connectionState=disconnected; game keeps running, seat reserved, turn timer + timeout auto-play unchanged) and deliver PLAYER_DISCONNECTED to the remaining humans. Client side: AppShell persists the screen (domino-poker-screen) in sessionStorage (NOT localStorage - fixed Phase 10.3: localStorage made mp-lobby a permanent landing default; sessionStorage survives a same-tab refresh for reconnect but a fresh session/tab always opens the single-player main lobby) so a refresh returns to mp-lobby and the MP client reconnects; MultiplayerClient does NOT reconnect on close code 4003 (superseded) to avoid a two-tab ping-pong. PublicSnapshot now includes turnId (= currentTurn?.turnId) for reconnect submit. Phase 9.3 'Exit during game' (forfeit): LEAVE_ROOM while IN_GAME -> handleForfeit -> rooms.forfeitInGame: dispatch core PLAYER_FORFEIT (new command -> applyPlayerForfeit sets the seat's MP status to 'bot' so the GameDirector auto-plays it instantly; emits PLAYER_LEFT) + LobbyManager.forfeitSeat (converts the seat to a bot, or destroys the room if no humans remain) + clears clientRoom membership (the player cannot rejoin) + disposeRoom on destroy. PublicSnapshot.isAI now reflects status==='bot' so a forfeited seat renders as AI. Crash-fix: RoomEngine.dispose() cancels the pending turn-timeout when a room is destroyed mid-game (a stray timeout firing on a removed engine used to crash the server), plus handleTurnTimeout guards on a missing director. Phase 9.3-b (abandoned-room destroy, DONE): when the last ONLINE human disconnects from an IN_GAME room, messageRouter.maybeScheduleAbandon checks GatewayHub.isOnline (-> SessionManager.hasActiveConnection) for the seated humans; if none are online it calls rooms.scheduleAbandonGrace(roomId, run) (RoomManager.abandonGraceMs, prod 30s, default 0 = disabled). restoreRoomOnReconnect cancels the grace when a human returns (so refresh survives); if the grace fires with still nobody online, destroyAbandonedRoom -> rooms.destroyRoom (lobby.destroyRoom + disposeRoom + clears clientRoom membership) + LOBBY_STATE broadcast. Explicit Exit (forfeit) destroys immediately when last human; disconnect uses the grace window.

Seq recovery (Phase 6.7): RoomEngine keeps a bounded ring-buffer of the last maxEventLog events (default 512, injectable for tests) and exposes getEventsSince(lastSeq): {mode:'incremental', events} when every event after lastSeq is still retained (lastSeq+1 >= oldest retained seq, or lastSeq===currentSeq -> empty), else {mode:'snapshot'} (lastSeq too old/evicted, negative, or ahead of server). RoomManager.getEventsSince(roomId, lastSeq?) returns snapshot mode when lastSeq is undefined. The router's REQUEST_SNAPSHOT (requireMembership then getEventsSince) replays only the missing GAME_EVENTs to the requester on incremental, or sends a full personalized STATE_SNAPSHOT otherwise - response goes to the requester only.

SUBMIT_BID/SUBMIT_MOVE routing (Phase 6.7): handleSubmitBid/handleSubmitMove share handleSubmit, whose server validation order is the transport layer (roomId membership via requireMembership + clientId->corePlayerId via corePlayerIdForClient) then the core/RoomEngine (current player, turnId match, deadline, requestId idempotency, legality). The client NEVER sends a playerId (the schema has none) so the acting player is authoritative from identity+seat. completeSubmit branches: idempotentReplay -> resync the requester with a STATE_SNAPSHOT only; rejected -> ERROR with mapped code (toMoveErrorCode: wrong_player/turn_not_started/turn_id_mismatch -> NOT_YOUR_TURN, ACTION_TOO_LATE -> ACTION_TOO_LATE, else MOVE_REJECTED) + echoed requestId; accepted -> advanceGame then deliverGameUpdate (GAME_EVENTs then a fresh personalized STATE_SNAPSHOT per seated human, so new secret hands after START_NEXT_ROUND are covered). guard() now echoes requestId on LobbyError.

START_GAME delivery (Phase 6.7): RoomManager holds a GameDirector per room; startGame creates engine+director (no advance, backward compatible) and advanceGame(roomId) runs the loop and returns events (reused after every human SUBMIT). The router's handleStartGame calls startGame+advanceGame+getSeq+getSeatedHumans, then delivers a personalized STATE_SNAPSHOT (per human via getSnapshotForClient) plus the opening GAME_EVENTs (incl. TURN_STARTED carrying the turnId needed for SUBMIT_BID) ONLY to seated humans via the new GatewayHub.sendToPlayer (game state/events never leak to lobby users outside the room), then pushes LOBBY_STATE. getSeatedHumans maps human seats -> {clientId, corePlayerId}; getSeq exposes the engine room-event seq for snapshot correlation; directors are cleaned up on room destroy/leave.

Lobby chat (Phase 6.6, src/chat/LobbyChat.ts) is a deliberately state-free in-memory service: it only handles {id, authorDisplayId, text, serverNow} and has NO access to RoomEngine/GameState/tiles. submit(authorPlayerId, authorDisplayId, rawText) trims then rejects empty->INVALID_MESSAGE and >maxLength(200)->INVALID_MESSAGE (length checked on trimmed pre-escape text so HTML escaping can't push a legal message over the limit), enforces a per-player TOKEN-BUCKET rate limit (Phase 10.3 scalability fix, replacing the old fixed 1500ms gap which rejected normal fast typing): burstCapacity default 5 messages in a row, refillMs default 2000 (~1 msg/2s sustained); only accepted sends consume a token; bucket state per playerId is cleaned up via forget(playerId) when the player FULLY disconnects (router.onDisconnected) so memory does not grow unbounded at scale -> RATE_LIMITED on empty bucket. Stores the RAW text (Phase 8: no server-side HTML-escaping - the client renders chat as React text, which is the XSS layer; server escaping double-escaped) into a ring buffer capped at historyLimit(50). The author is the server displayId (client cannot spoof). Phase 10.3 persistence: onMessage observer pushes accepted messages to the DB (MatchPersistence) and hydrate() preloads the buffer from DB at startup so CHAT_HISTORY survives a server restart. CoreMessageRouter SEND_CHAT broadcasts CHAT_MESSAGE on success (ERROR with echoed requestId on reject); the router lifecycle hooks onConnected (sends CHAT_HISTORY to the newcomer + broadcasts LOBBY_STATE for the bumped onlineCount) and onDisconnected (forget chat bucket + broadcasts LOBBY_STATE) are called by the gateway. SCALE NOTE: this is a single GLOBAL lobby chat broadcast to all clients. Phase 11 load testing exposed a broadcast-fanout OOM at ~1000 clients (every CHAT_MESSAGE + LOBBY_STATE fanned out to all N sockets; ws outbound buffers grew unbounded -> 3.8GB heap OOM). HARDENED with three layers: (1) LOBBY_STATE debounce/coalescing in CoreMessageRouter (lobbyStateDebounceMs, prod 200ms; default 0 keeps tests synchronous) so hundreds of room-change broadcasts collapse to one per window; (2) slow-client backpressure in WebSocketGateway.deliver (skip a send when conn.bufferedAmount() > slowClientBufferCap, default 1MB) so memory stays bounded - skipped game clients resync via REQUEST_SNAPSHOT seq-gap, skipped lobby/chat is non-critical; (3) broadcast pre-serialization - WebSocketGateway.broadcast JSON.stringify's the event ONCE and sends the same string to all via GatewayConnection.sendSerialized (wsTransport ws.send(payload); fallback to send(event) for fakes), eliminating N redundant stringifies per fanout. PostgreSQL mode now adds PostgresEventBus for cross-instance broadcast/player/supersede fanout. Validated: 100=~86MB, 500=~100MB/RTT63ms/no-leak, 1000=~120MB/0-dropped/survives (RTT saturated ~3s on one event loop, halved by pre-serialize).

Lobby routing (CoreMessageRouter) takes RoomManager and RouteContext {identity, conn, hub, serverNow}. Mapping: LIST_ROOMS->ROOM_LIST; CREATE_ROOM({visibility,numberOfRounds,fillWithBots?})->ROOM_CREATED+LOBBY_STATE, optionally filling bots immediately; JOIN_ROOM(roomId|code)->ROOM_JOINED+LOBBY_STATE; LEAVE_ROOM->ROOM_LEFT+LOBBY_STATE; FILL_SEATS_WITH_BOTS->ROOM_JOINED(room-view refresh)+LOBBY_STATE. identity.playerId is the RoomManager membership key (== HELLO clientId). JOIN seatIndex is currently ignored. RoomManager LobbyError is caught in guard() and mapped to ProtocolErrorCode.

name: Documentation
purpose: Public README, license, screenshots, and human-readable rules, scoring examples, and strategy notes for Domino Poker. docs/SCALING.md (Phase 11) is the plain-language scaling guide: it records the measured load-test bottleneck (global chat broadcast fanout on a single Node event loop - proven NOT to be DB/games/memory via a gameFraction=0 experiment), the existing defenses (token-bucket rate-limit + LOBBY_STATE debounce + slow-client backpressure + broadcast pre-serialization), and the scale path. PostgreSQL mode now implements the first multi-instance backplane in code: room leases, durable sessions, and PostgresEventBus fanout; Redis/NATS remain possible future replacements only if PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY becomes too small for production traffic. docs/DEPLOYMENT.md (Phase 12.1) is the plain-language VPS (Hostinger KVM 4) prep guide: production build (npm ci && npm run build), .env config, and BOTH run options - a systemd service (deploy/domino-poker.service, recommended, with a plain explanation of what systemd is) and a Docker alternative (deploy/Dockerfile + root .dockerignore). Phase 12.2 adds reverse-proxy examples (deploy/nginx.conf.example with map $http_upgrade + proxy_read_timeout 3600s for long WS; deploy/Caddyfile.example with auto-TLS/auto-upgrade) and a DEPLOYMENT.md section 4 section: one public domain on 443 splits to /ws -> 127.0.0.1:4000 (MP server) and / -> 127.0.0.1:3000 (Next.js web); behind a proxy SERVER_HOST=127.0.0.1 and the web MUST be built with NEXT_PUBLIC_MP_WS_URL=wss://domain/ws (else the client derives ws://host:4000/ws which bypasses the proxy). /metrics should not be exposed publicly. PostgreSQL persistence is now implemented in code: `DATABASE_URL=postgres://...` routes `openStorage` to `PostgresStorage` (`pg`) with JSONB payloads, BIGINT ms timestamps, ON CONFLICT idempotency, atomic player-stat increments, durable sessions, room leases, and fanout bus tables; SQLite remains the default for local file paths. Phase 12 (12.1 config+build+service, 12.2 reverse proxy, 12.3 DB strategy) is COMPLETE. config.ts gained SERVER_HOST (bind addr, default 0.0.0.0) + NODE_ENV + SERVER_PORT (alias of HTTP_PORT) and index.ts binds server.listen(port, host); .env.example documents all vars. TURN_DURATION_MS is now wired (config -> RoomManager.turnDurationMs -> CREATE_GAME command -> gameSetup metadata -> MultiplayerGameState.turnDurationMs -> applyStartTurn deadlineAt); the old hardcoded const in applyCommand.ts is gone. Default 10000, validated [100,600000]; it does NOT affect shuffling/dealing (those depend only on seed), so determinism is preserved.
risks: Some documentation may lag behind tested code behavior.
Use docs as reference material, not as proof that implementation is current.

docs/SCALING.md, docs/DEPLOYMENT.md, docs/DB_MIGRATION.md and docs/mockups/* are intentionally git-IGNORED (local-only, per .gitignore + commit efa63fc 'keep only game rules/strategy public'). Only the four game-rules/strategy docs are tracked. Do NOT link the ignored docs from the public README (they 404 on GitHub); keep them as local references only.

name: Local Runtime Data
purpose: Local runtime data directory for the default live multiplayer SQLite database (path from DATABASE_URL when it is a file path). PostgreSQL deployments use an external database URL instead.
risks: Runtime SQLite files must stay ignored and must not be committed.
Persistence is live (match event log + player stats + chat history); do not treat this directory as an unused placeholder.

name: Multiplayer Simulators
purpose: Workspace (`@domino-poker/simulators`) that drives full multiplayer games through `applyCommand` with random legal bids and moves, plus optional timeoutProbability (TURN_TIMEOUT auto-actions) and disconnectProbability (PLAYER_DISCONNECT/PLAYER_RESUME churn, where disconnected players' turns time out), to stress-test rules, determinism, and invariants before networking. Backs the `npm run simulate` command.
risks: Imports MP only through `@domino-poker/core/multiplayer`; must never reach into single-player APIs or duplicate shuffle/RNG logic (it reuses createSeededRng from the MP zone).

Determinism contract: same seed must reproduce the same game; randomness must come only from the seeded MP RNG, never Math.random or Date.now (the CLI may use Date.now solely for timing logs, not decisions). Timeout and disconnect decisions each use a separate seeded stream (`${seed}:timeouts`, `${seed}:disconnect`) so enabling either never disturbs the bid/move decision stream.
The harness asserts invariants after every command and enforces single-active-turn plus monotonic turnId; keep these checks intact.

typecheck/test resolve `@domino-poker/core/multiplayer` to core source (tsconfig paths + vitest alias) so no core build is needed; the build/simulate path resolves to built dist, so `npm run simulate` builds core first.

Phase 4.2 volume gate: runVolumeSuite runs a 4-scenario mix (plain/timeout/disconnect/combined); volume.test.ts defaults to 2500/scenario = 10000 games (~16s) and is overridable with SIM_VOLUME; the CLI runs it via SIM_SUITE=1. This is part of Gate 1 core_ready, which is now complete.

name: Multiplayer Load Test
purpose: Workspace (`@domino-poker/load-test`, Phase 11) that generates LOCAL load against a running MP server by speaking the REAL WebSocket protocol via Node `ws` (HELLO -> lobby/chat -> later game commands), so it exercises the actual server path (gateway -> router -> RoomEngine -> timers -> persistence) under concurrency rather than mocking internals. Backs `npm run load:local`. CHUNK 1 (done): metrics.ts (pure percentile/summarizeLatencies + LoadMetrics aggregator), VirtualClient, runLoadTest CLI with /health liveness. CHUNK 2a (done): VirtualClient gained game actions (createRoomWithBots -> startGame; gameplay load via the agreed bot-fill + server turn-timeout (default 10s, now TURN_DURATION_MS-configurable) auto-play design, so NO game rules are duplicated in the tool) and reconnect() (deliberate socket drop + re-HELLO with reconnectToken, counted as reconnect not dropped); the CLI now spawns a gameFraction of bot-filled games + a churnEvery disconnect/reconnect wave, and polls the server /metrics endpoint to report server peak RSS, CPU% (computed from cumulative cpuUser+cpuSystem micros deltas), and peak connections. The server gained GET /metrics (httpServer.ts: process RSS/heap/cpuUsage/uptime + injected connectionCount=gateway.onlineCount()). CHUNK 2b (done): all three levels validated - 100 (~86MB), 500 (~100MB/RTT63ms), 1000 (~120MB/0-dropped/survives). The 1000 level surfaced a broadcast-fanout OOM, fixed by debounce + backpressure + broadcast pre-serialization (see Multiplayer Server chat scale note). runLoadTest verdict was refined to separate STABILITY (survivedLoad via awaitServerRecovery retry-on-/health + 0 dropped sockets = the real pass/fail) from LATENCY/saturation (high mean RTT / connectFailures = informational warning, not a crash); clientIds carry a per-run id so re-runs against a non-restarted server don't collide with durable reconnect tokens. Phase 11 acceptance + Gate 4 'pamata slodzes tests' are ticked. REMAINING per the user's added TODO: strip any throwaway test code at Phase 11 close (the load-test tool itself stays as a deliverable).
risks: It is a TOOL/load generator, not the MP server determinism path, so Date.now()/randomness are allowed here (unlike the server/core MP zone). It must never import single-player APIs.

It speaks the protocol only through @domino-poker/shared contracts (no duplicated message shapes); typecheck resolves shared to src via tsconfig paths + vitest alias, build resolves to dist via a project reference, so load:local builds shared first.
Server RSS/CPU is NOT yet measured (Chunk 2 adds a /metrics endpoint); the report's peak RSS is the GENERATOR process, clearly labelled. Liveness/crash is detected via /health polling.
Requires a running MP server (npm run dev:server); the CLI aborts early if /health is unreachable. Run it against a throwaway DATABASE_URL so load chat traffic does not pollute dev.sqlite.
